title: Merging subitem timeline/date column to parent timeline/date column
excerpt: Hello, This is a request to merge subitem timeline/date column value to parent timeline/date column. In this use case, some projects have only single release (parent item) while some projects have multi releases (subitem…

posts.author: mamta (Mamta Madhusudan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, This is a request to merge subitem timeline/date column value to parent timeline/date column. In this use case, some projects have only single release (parent item) while some projects have multi releases (subitems).

We want to add the timeline/date to parent item for single line items and for projects with subitems we want to add the timeline/date at the subitem that should merge into the same column as parent timeline/date column. I am not looking to add summary of sub item as that will create another column.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Mamta Madhusudan:

In this use case, some projects have only single release (parent item) while some projects have multi releases (subitems).

Hi @mamta

The app Rollup Subitems (see monday.com: Apps Marketplace) can do that. It summarizes the subitems timeline into to parent item. Different for the option “Show summary in parent” is that the app write to a normal timeline column in the parent. The same column can be used to enter the timeline when the item does not have subitems.

posts.cookedHtml:

The app Rollup Subitems (see monday.com: Apps Marketplace) can do that. It summarizes the subitems timeline into to parent item. Different for the option “Show summary in parent” is that the app write to a normal timeline column in the parent. The same column can be used to enter the timeline wh

Hi Bas, is this a third party app? We cannot use any third party apps in our organization. Thank you.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: This a an app from the monday marketplace developed by a third party. Your monday experience will be limited without using any apps from the marketplace.

posts.author: Paul_starcon (Paul)
posts.cookedHtml: That seems to be the problem in itself. The amount of add-ons you need to perform basic functions available on other programs, is the reason many of my colleagues are leaving Monday.com. Also, most of these add-ons are becoming available only with a paid monthly subscription.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Paul_starcon & @mamta

We developed an app called subitem automations that provides robust automation capabilities to subitems. Here’s an example of how it can be applied in this case →

Screenshot 2024-12-27 at 16.00.332086×596 36.1 KB

Hope that helps!
